reason_for_decision: The article passes because it translates raw multi-sensor alerting into a concrete operator-queue model with strong workflow and measurement guidance.
strengths: The article clearly separates machine-originated alerts from operator-owned queue items, which gives the piece a strong and useful framing.
It adds real operational depth through sections on deduplication, state models, ownership, evidence packaging, and queue metrics rather than stopping at generic alerting advice.
The queue-health discussion gives buyers and platform designers concrete metrics that are more useful than alert-count reporting.
summary_assessment: This is a strong system-design article. It is practical, technically credible, and highly useful for readers designing control-room workflow rather than just integrating sensor feeds.
